chapter_context.summary: Hannah's desperate prayer for a son, the birth and dedication of Samuel to the service of the LORD at Shiloh.

outline.text: Elkanah and His Two Wives; Hannah's Sorrow
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Elkanah and His Two Wives; Hannah's Sorrow (1:1-8), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: Hannah's Vow and Prayer at Shiloh; Eli's Comfort
outline.explanation: This section focuses on Hannah's Vow and Prayer at Shiloh; Eli's Comfort (1:9-18), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

outline.text: The Birth and Dedication of Samuel
outline.explanation: This section focuses on The Birth and Dedication of Samuel (1:19-28), highlighting its theological significance and showing how it develops the central message of 1 Samuel.

key_themes.title: Sovereignty of God
key_themes.description: God raises up the humble (Hannah, Samuel, David) and humbles the proud (Eli's sons, Saul).

key_themes.title: The Heart vs. Outward Appearance
key_themes.description: God looks at the inner spiritual character, not physical stature or social status.

devotional.question: Reflecting on the events of Chapter 1, how does this encourage you to trust God when circumstances look bleak?
How does this chapter prompt you to examine the sincerity of your own heart before the LORD?

cultural_historical.title: Transition to Monarchy
cultural_historical.text: Israel's request for a king represents a major cultural shift from a tribal league led by temporary judges to a centralized dynastic monarchy.
